sections.paragraph:
Σκευᾶς, ᾶ, ὁ (Plut., Caes. 715 [16, 2]; Appian, Bell. Civ. 2, 60 §247 [a centurion: Lat. Scaeva]; Cass. Dio 56, 16, 1; B-D-F §125, 2) Sceva a high priest Ac 19:14 (acc. to EKase, Am. Hist. Review 43, ’38, 437f a misunderstanding due to dittography).—BMastin, JTS 27, ’76, 405–12 (w. ref. to CIG 2889, on which s. LRobert, Les gladiateurs dans l’orient grec ’40, 180ff: an adj. [σκαιός] meaning ‘left-handed’); BHHW III 1813; Pauly-W. Suppl. IV 931f; II 471–83; Hemer, Acts 234.—M-M.

Plut Plut , I–II a.d.—List 5

Appian Appian , I–II a.d.—List 5

Lat. Lat. = Latin

B-D-F B-D-F = FBlass, ADebrunner, RFunk, A Greek Grammar of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature—List 6

acc. to acc. to = according to

Hist.

Hist. (after a personal name)=historicus, historian

hist. = historian, historical

JTS JTS = Journal of Theological Studies—List 6

ref. ref. = reference(s)

CIG CIG = Corpus Inscriptionum Graecarum—List 3

adj. adj. = adjective

BHHW BHHW = Biblisch-historisches Handwörterbuch—List 6

Pauly-W Pauly-W = AP./GW., Real-Encyclopädie der klassischen Altertumswissenschaft—List 6

Suppl.

Suppl. = Supplement (to serials)

suppl. = supplement, supplied (in grammatical references)

Hemer, Acts Hemer, Acts = CH., The Book of Acts in the Setting of Hellenistic History—List 6

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 927
